news.headline: White House Launches 'Freedom Fuel' Gas Stations — Patriotism Now Available at the Pump for $3.47
news.summary: ホワイトハウスが「フリーダム・フューエル」ブランドのガソリンスタンドを発表。全国平均より1ガロン約30セント安い3.47ドルで販売するとしている。

commentary: 矛盾: 「政府は市場に介入しない」と標榜する政権が、ガソリンに国家ブランドを冠して価格競争に乗り出すという、自由市場原理との絶妙な矛盾を体現している。
滑稽さ: 愛国心をガソリンスタンドの看板に落とし込み、「自由の燃料」という名前で給油できる日が来るとは、建国の父たちも想定していなかっただろう。
日本・海外から見ると: 日本でも政府の燃料補助金制度はあるが、それに「自由」や「愛国」と名前をつけてブランド化する発想は新鮮すぎて、むしろ国営企業の広告のように映る。
imagePrompts: A gleaming gas station with an oversized patriotic red-white-and-blue logo reading 'Freedom Fuel,' an American flag on every pump, a suited government official cutting a ribbon in front, and a price sign showing $3.47 while a competitor's station visible in the background shows $3.79
A gas pump nozzle shaped like the Statue of Liberty's torch, with a fuel receipt printing out that reads 'Thank you for choosing Freedom — have a patriotic day'

A government press briefing podium set up in the middle of a gas station forecourt, surrounded by reporters and idling cars, with a giant banner overhead reading 'FREEDOM FUEL NETWORK — OFFICIAL LAUNCH'
captions: The White House announced 'Freedom Fuel' stations selling gas at $3.47 a gallon — because nothing says free market like the government setting your price at the pump.
They said they'd bring down energy costs. They just didn't mention they'd rename gasoline first.
For years politicians promised cheaper gas. Turns out all it needed was a rebrand and a press release.
You can now purchase Freedom by the gallon. Liberty is still sold separately, and Premium is extra.
The invisible hand of the market just got a very visible government logo on it.
captionsJa: ホワイトハウスが「フリーダム・フューエル」の1ガロン3.47ドルを発表——自由市場を掲げる政権が、政府公認の価格でガソリンを売り始めるとは。
エネルギーコスト削減を約束していた。まさかガソリンの名前を変えるだけで達成するとは思っていなかったが。
長年、政治家たちは安いガソリンを約束してきた。必要だったのはブランド名とプレスリリースだけだったらしい。
自由を1ガロン単位で購入できる時代が来た。リバティは別売り、プレミアムは割増料金。
市場の「見えざる手」に、政府の大きなロゴがついた。
candidates.newsEn: The White House announced the launch of the Freedom Fuel Network, a gasoline retail initiative offering fuel at $3.47 per gallon. The price is advertised as more than 30 cents below the national average. The branding aligns with the administration's broader push to market energy policy under patriotic slogans.

ironyEn.contradiction: An administration philosophically opposed to government market intervention just launched a government-branded gas station chain to undercut private competitors.
ironyEn.absurdity: You can now fill your tank with 'Freedom' — which, at $3.47 a gallon, turns out to be about 30 cents cheaper than regular liberty.
ironyEn.outside: From abroad, this looks less like deregulation and more like a state oil company with a better logo.

ironyEn.contradiction: The free market is sacred — unless the White House can slap a brand on it and hold a ribbon-cutting ceremony.
ironyEn.absurdity: The Founding Fathers fought for life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness. Apparently the happiness part requires a loyalty rewards card.
ironyEn.outside: Japan subsidizes fuel quietly through bureaucratic transfers. America does it with a brand name, a press release, and presumably a bald eagle on the sign.

news.headline: Meta's New Smart Glasses Would Record Everything — Because You Clearly Haven't Given Us Enough Data
news.summary: Metaが常時録音・数秒ごとに写真を撮影する「スーパーセンシング」スマートグラスのプロトタイプを開発中だと報じられた。装着者はMetaのAIに撮影内容について質問できる。

commentary: 矛盾: プライバシー侵害で繰り返し議会に呼ばれてきたMetaが、今度は「ユーザー自身が常時録画する」デバイスを提供することで、データ収集の責任をユーザーに転嫁しようとしている。
滑稽さ: 「何も見逃したくない」という現代人の欲望を極限まで突き詰めた結果、自分の人生を自分でリアルタイム監視するガジェットが生まれた。
日本・海外から見ると: 日本では街中に防犯カメラが多いとはいえ、個人が周囲の全員を無断で常時録画するメガネを日常的にかけて歩く社会は、また別次元の話だと感じるだろう。
imagePrompts: A person wearing sleek smart glasses at a dinner table while everyone around them has small recording-light indicators reflected in their eyes, with a floating data cloud above the glasses labeled 'uploading memories'
A product display case showing smart glasses next to a small print terms-of-service document that unfurls to cover the entire floor of the store
A split image: on the left, a person happily wearing the glasses on a city street; on the right, a vast server farm with the same city street rendered in tiny data points on a monitor
captions: Meta is developing glasses that record everything around you at all times — finally, a way to share your entire life with a corporation that already knows your birthday, your ex, and your 2 a.m. search history.
The glasses snap a photo every few seconds so you never miss a moment — and neither does the data center in Menlo Park.
Experts warn the always-on glasses raise serious privacy concerns. Meta calls those experts 'early adopters who haven't tried the product yet.'
You can ask the AI what you just saw, which is impressive, unless you consider that human eyes were doing this for free for 300,000 years.
Meta says the glasses will help you remember everything. Presumably including the moment you agreed to the terms and conditions.
captionsJa: Metaが常時録画メガネを開発中——自分の誕生日も元恋人も深夜の検索履歴も既に知っている企業に、ついに日常の全映像も渡せるようになる。
数秒おきに写真を撮るので、大切な瞬間を見逃さない。メンローパークのデータセンターも見逃さない。
専門家たちは常時録画メガネの深刻なプライバシー問題を警告している。Metaは彼らを「まだ製品を試していない初期ユーザー候補」と呼んでいる。
「今何を見たか」をAIに聞ける機能が搭載される。ちなみに人間の目は30万年間、無料でそれをやり続けてきた。
Metaは「すべてを記憶できる」と言う。利用規約に同意した瞬間も含めて、きっちり。
candidates.newsEn: According to the Financial Times, Meta is developing prototype smart glasses with 'super sensing' capabilities that would continuously record audio and capture photos every few seconds. The wearer could query Meta AI about the captured footage. The always-on design would make the device one of the most pervasive AI wearables yet proposed by a major tech company.

ironyEn.contradiction: Meta has been hauled before Congress multiple times for privacy violations — so naturally their next product lets you personally surveil everyone around you, which somehow makes it your problem, not theirs.
ironyEn.absurdity: The glasses record audio continuously and snap photos every few seconds, so you'll finally have evidence of everything you've ever done — and so will Meta.
ironyEn.outside: In most countries, secretly recording people in public is legally murky at best. Meta's solution: make the recording device fashionable.

ironyEn.contradiction: A company whose terms of service already own your digital life is now building hardware to capture your physical one, frame by frame.
ironyEn.absurdity: You can ask the AI 'what did I just see?' — a question humans previously answered using their own brains, which were, crucially, not monetizable.
ironyEn.outside: From outside the US, this reads as the logical endpoint of a culture that decided convenience was worth more than privacy a long time ago.

news.headline: White House World Cup Czar Defends Trump Lobbying FIFA Over Red Card — Diplomacy Has Entered the Group Stage
news.summary: ホワイトハウスのワールドカップ担当責任者アンドリュー・ジュリアーニ氏が、トランプ大統領によるFIFAへのロビー活動を擁護した。米国選手フォラリン・バロガン選手の退場処分取り消しを求めるもの。

commentary: 矛盾: スポーツの政治介入を批判することで知られるFIFAに対し、ホワイトハウスが公式に選手の処分取り消しをロビー活動するという、「スポーツの政治化を防げ」という建前と真逆の行動に出ている。
滑稽さ: ホワイトハウスに「ワールドカップ担当チーム」が存在し、その責任者が記者会見で審判の判定を「非常に非常に怪しい」と分析するシーン自体が、既にコメディの域に達している。
日本・海外から見ると: 他国の政府がFIFAの判定に公式ロビーをかけたら国際問題になりかねないが、開催国の大統領がやると「外交」と呼ばれるあたり、ルールの適用は一様ではないと映る。
imagePrompts: A formal White House press briefing room where the podium has a soccer ball on it and a giant TV screen behind the briefer shows a freeze-frame of a red card being shown, with officials in suits pointing at the screen with laser pointers
A referee on a soccer field holding up a red card while in the background a limousine with government plates pulls onto the pitch and a figure in a suit leans out the window waving diplomatic papers
A government org chart pinned to a wall showing 'National Security Council,' 'Council of Economic Advisers,' and below them, 'White House World Cup Task Force — Red Card Division'
captions: The White House confirmed it lobbied FIFA over a red card — because when you're running two wars and a trade dispute, nothing says 'executive priorities' like a soccer suspension appeal.
Andrew Giuliani called the referee's decision 'very, very highly suspicious,' which is the most diplomatic thing a White House official has said about anything in months.
The US has an official White House World Cup task force. Its first major diplomatic achievement: arguing with a referee.
FIFA rules prohibit government interference in football. Fortunately, the White House has a task force specifically for things FIFA prohibits.
In a surprise twist, the most detailed foreign policy statement of the week concerned a red card shown in the 67th minute.
captionsJa: ホワイトハウスがFIFAに退場処分取り消しをロビーしたと確認——2つの戦争と貿易摩擦を抱えながら、今週最も力を入れた外交案件がサッカーの懲戒処分だった。
アンドリュー・ジュリアーニ氏は審判の判定を「非常に非常に怪しい」と評した。ここ数ヶ月でホワイトハウス高官が発した最も外交的な発言である。
米国にはワールドカップ担当のホワイトハウス公式チームがある。その最初の主要外交成果は、審判への異議申し立てだった。
FIFAの規定は政府のサッカーへの介入を禁じている。幸い、ホワイトハウスにはFIFAが禁じていることを専門に扱うタスクフォースがある。
今週最も詳細な外交政策声明が、67分に提示されたレッドカードに関するものだったとは、誰も予想していなかった。
candidates.newsEn: Andrew Giuliani, the head of the White House's World Cup task force, publicly defended President Trump's lobbying of FIFA to lift the suspension of US player Folarin Balogun, who received a red card during a match. Giuliani described the referee's decision as 'very, very highly suspicious.' The intervention marks an unusual use of executive branch influence in an international sporting body's disciplinary process.

ironyEn.contradiction: FIFA exists partly to keep governments out of football — yet here is an official White House task force holding press briefings to critique referee decisions on behalf of the host nation's team.
ironyEn.absurdity: The United States has a formal government office dedicated to a soccer tournament, and that office's first major public act was disputing a yellow-card upgrade with a global governing body.
ironyEn.outside: If any other nation's head of state called FIFA to lobby for their player's reinstatement, it would be an international scandal. When the host country does it, it gets a task force and a press conference.

ironyEn.contradiction: The administration that frequently rails against international bodies overstepping their authority is now demanding that an international body overrule its own referee.
ironyEn.absurdity: Andrew Giuliani, whose job title is 'White House World Cup Head,' went on record to call a red card 'very, very highly suspicious' — a sentence that will look incredible on a government résumé.
ironyEn.outside: International observers note that FIFA's rules explicitly prohibit government interference in football — a rule that is, apparently, also subject to negotiation.

news.headline: 'We Cannot Choose to Become Idiots': Brown University's AI Cheating Scandal Proves We Can, Actually
news.summary: ブラウン大学でAIを使った不正行為が問題化し、担当教授が「AIによる不正は失敗した社会につながる」と警告した。

commentary: 矛盾: 大学が高額な授業料でAIリテラシー教育を売り込む一方で、学生はそのAIを使って課題をスキップしており、教育機関が自ら需要を作り出した問題に直面している。
滑稽さ: 「わたしたちは自分からバカになることを選べない」と教授は言ったが、AI企業が数十億ドルを投じて「選べる」ようにした結果がこれである。
日本・海外から見ると: 日本の大学でもAI利用のルール整備が急務だが、「AIに課題をやらせることは自分をバカにする選択だ」という倫理的フレームで学生を説得できるかは、また別の問題だ。
imagePrompts: A grand ivy-covered university lecture hall where every student is staring at a laptop, the professor at the podium is also staring at a laptop, and a large banner on the wall reads 'WE CANNOT CHOOSE TO BECOME IDIOTS' while a small robot hand is visible under each desk typing away
A diploma being handed to a student at a graduation ceremony, but the diploma reads 'Bachelor of Prompting' with a small AI logo as the university seal

A library study room where a student sits at a desk with a mountain of textbooks, all of them closed and stacked as a footrest, while a single laptop screen glows with an AI chat interface and a completed essay
captions: Brown University's professor declared 'we cannot choose to become idiots' — which is technically true, but OpenAI spent billions making sure we don't have to.
The AI cheating scandal at Brown raises a profound question: if the AI does your homework, earns your degree, and gets your job, at what point does it start paying your student loans?
Experts say AI cheating leads to 'a failed society.' The AI, when asked, produced a well-sourced five-paragraph essay disagreeing.
Brown University is cracking down on students who use AI to write their essays. The AI has drafted a formal response expressing deep concern.
The professor said students are choosing to become idiots. The students said they're choosing to be efficient. Somewhere in the middle is a $90,000 tuition bill.
captionsJa: ブラウン大学の教授は「私たちは自分からバカになることを選べない」と言った——技術的には正しいが、OpenAIは数十億ドルをかけて「その必要がない」状況を作り出した。
AIが宿題をやり、学位を取り、仕事に就くなら、次は学生ローンを返済し始めるのはいつか——というのが今回のスキャンダルの本質的な問いである。
専門家は「AIによる不正は失敗した社会につながる」と言う。そのAIに意見を求めたところ、引用付きの五段落の反論エッセイが出てきた。
ブラウン大学はAIで論文を書く学生を取り締まっている。AIはすでに正式な懸念表明文書の草稿を用意した。
教授は「学生は自らバカになることを選んでいる」と言い、学生は「効率を選んでいる」と言う。その中間には90,000ドルの授業料がある。
candidates.newsEn: Brown University is at the center of an AI cheating scandal that has prompted faculty to speak out. One professor declared that using AI to cheat leads to 'a failed society' and that students cannot 'choose to become idiots.' The incident reflects a broader crisis in higher education over academic integrity as AI tools become increasingly accessible and capable.

ironyEn.contradiction: Universities spent years arguing that expensive tuition buys access to cutting-edge technology and skills — then were surprised when students used cutting-edge technology to skip the skills part.
ironyEn.absurdity: A professor at one of America's most elite institutions declared 'we cannot choose to become idiots' — a statement that required no AI to generate, and yet somehow feels like the most human response possible.
ironyEn.outside: From outside the US, it's notable that the same Silicon Valley culture celebrated on university donor walls produced the tools now dismantling university academic culture.

ironyEn.contradiction: Higher education sells itself as the path to intellectual development, then discovers that the intellectual development can be outsourced for free to a chatbot.
ironyEn.absurdity: The scandal is called an AI cheating crisis, but technically the AI isn't cheating — it's doing exactly what it was designed to do. The crisis is that it's very good at it.
ironyEn.outside: Internationally, this is being watched as a preview: whatever solution Brown finds, or fails to find, will be the test case for every university on earth.

news.headline: Apple Pledges $30 Billion to Broadcom for US Chipmaking — Patriotism, Apparently, Has a Price Tag
news.summary: アップルがブロードコムとの半導体製造パートナーシップを300億ドル以上に拡大すると発表。同社史上最大規模のアメリカ国内製造コミットメントとなる。

commentary: 矛盾: 数十年にわたって「グローバルサプライチェーンの最適化」を理由に製造を海外に移してきたAppleが、政治的圧力が高まった途端に「史上最大の国内製造コミットメント」を発表するタイミングの妙。
滑稽さ: 300億ドルの愛国心とはなかなかの値段だが、iPhoneの組み立て自体はまだ海外なので、「アメリカ製」の定義については引き続き創造的な解釈が続く。
日本・海外から見ると: 日本でも半導体の国内回帰は政策課題だが、企業が自発的にやるのではなく、政治的圧力で数字を出すパターンは、産業政策と広報の境界線をあいまいにする。
imagePrompts: A giant check for $30,000,000,000 made out to 'American Manufacturing' being handed by a figure in a sleek black turtleneck to Uncle Sam, who is holding it up to the light suspiciously while a factory building in the background has a small 'under construction' sign on it

A timeline graphic on a wall showing 'Apple Manufacturing Location' with a line running through decades labeled 'efficiency,' 'optimization,' 'globalization,' and then a sudden sharp turn upward at the end labeled 'tariffs'

An Apple Store display case where every product has a small flag tag — the chip has a US flag, but the phone, the screen, and the packaging have a row of other national flags — and a sales associate is pointing at the chip tag with a proud smile
captions: Apple announced its largest-ever US manufacturing commitment at $30 billion — which is a lot of patriotism, or roughly what it costs when the alternative is a 145% tariff.
The deal covers chipmaking, not iPhone assembly, so the most American component of your next iPhone will remain the $1,299 price tag.
Apple called it a historic commitment to American manufacturing. Economists called it a rational response to trade policy. Both are correct, which is somehow more depressing.
For decades, Apple said manufacturing abroad was about quality and efficiency. Apparently $30 billion and a tariff schedule also produce quality and efficiency.
Apple's press release used the word 'commitment' eleven times. The word 'tariff' appeared zero times, which itself took some engineering.
captionsJa: Appleが300億ドルの国内製造コミットメントを発表——かなりの愛国心だが、関税145%という代替案を考えると、むしろリーズナブルな選択とも言える。
この契約が対象とするのは半導体であり、iPhone本体の組み立てではない。つまり次のiPhoneで最も「アメリカ製」な部分は、1,299ドルという価格だ。
Appleは「歴史的な製造コミットメント」と呼んだ。エコノミストたちは「貿易政策への合理的な対応」と呼んだ。どちらも正しく、それがなぜか余計に虚しい。
数十年間、Appleは「品質と効率のために海外製造を選ぶ」と言い続けた。どうやら300億ドルと関税スケジュールも、品質と効率を生み出すらしい。
Appleのプレスリリースには「コミットメント」という言葉が11回登場した。「関税」という言葉はゼロ回で、これ自体がある種の技術的成果だ。
candidates.newsEn: Apple announced a $30 billion-plus expansion of its chipmaking agreement with Broadcom, representing its largest American manufacturing commitment to date. The deal is part of Apple's broader push to increase domestic production amid ongoing political pressure to reshore semiconductor manufacturing to the United States. The partnership focuses on components produced within the US.

ironyEn.contradiction: Apple spent decades perfecting the art of manufacturing everything abroad while calling it 'global efficiency' — and is now announcing its largest-ever domestic manufacturing commitment, which became patriotic just as the tariff environment made it financially sensible.
ironyEn.absurdity: Thirty billion dollars is a remarkable sum to spend on American manufacturing for a product still assembled overseas, but it does make for an excellent press release headline.
ironyEn.outside: From abroad, this looks like what happens when a government stops asking nicely and starts setting tariffs: suddenly corporate patriotism becomes very affordable.

ironyEn.contradiction: The same company that once called supply chain globalization an irreversible economic force is now leading the charge on reshoring — right on schedule with the political calendar.
ironyEn.absurdity: Apple's 'largest American manufacturing commitment' involves chips, not the phone itself — so the most American part of your iPhone remains the price.
ironyEn.outside: Countries trying to attract semiconductor investment are watching carefully: apparently the formula is tariffs, public pressure, and waiting for a company to need a good news cycle.
